title: viola tricolor subsp. parvula
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. parvula, also known as the small or dwarf pansy, is a subspecies of the common pansy plant (Viola tricolor) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. parvula include its ability to soothe sore throats, relieve colds and flu, and calm digestive issues. In herbal preparations, it is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also added to salves and creams for topical application.
